code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples into a single fluent sentence.
    Uses natural‑language templates for known predicates and joins
    all information about the same subject together when possible.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Simple template mapping for predicates
    templates = {
        "architecturalStyle": lambda s, o: f"{s} features {o.lower()}",
        "buildingStartDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} began construction in {o.strip('\"')}",
        "completionDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was completed in {o}",
        "floorCount": lambda s, o: f"{s} has {o} floors",
        "location": lambda s, o: f"{s} is located at {o}",
        "cost": lambda s, o: f"{s} cost {o}",
        "floorArea": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a floor area of {o} square metres",
        "architect": lambda s, o: f"{s} was designed by {o}",
        "owner": lambda s, o: f"{s} is owned by {o}",
        "formerName": lambda s, o: f"{s} was formerly known as {o}",
        "height": lambda s, o: f"{s} stands {o} metres tall",
        "buildingType": lambda s, o: f"{s} is a {o.lower()}",
        "developer": lambda s, o: f"{s} was developed by {o}",
        "tenant": lambda s, o: f"{s} is tenanted by {o}",
        "isPartOf": lambda s, o: f"{s} is part of {o}",
        "country": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in {o}",
        "currentTenants": lambda s, o: f"{s} currently houses {o}",
        "address": lambda s, o: f"{s} is situated at {o}",
        "inaugurationDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was inaugurated on {o.strip('\"')}",
        "birthPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born in {o}",
        "deathPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} died in {o}",
        "bedCount": lambda s, o: f"{s} has {o} beds",
        "region": lambda s, o: f"{s} lies in the {o} region",
        "state": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in {o}",
        "website": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s website is {o}",
        "yearOfConstruction": lambda s, o: f"{s} was built in {o}",
        "NationalRegisterOfHistoricPlacesReferenceNumber": lambda s, o: f"{s} has NRHP reference number {o}",
        "addedToTheNationalRegisterOfHistoricPlaces": lambda s, o: f"{s} was added to the NRHP on {o}",
        "significantBuilding": lambda s, o: f"{s} is known for the {o}",
        "governingBody": lambda s, o: f"{s} is governed by {o}",
        "leader": lambda s, o: f"{s} is led by {o}",
        "chancellor": lambda s, o: f"{s} has chancellor {o}",
        "governmentType": lambda s, o: f"{s} operates under a {o.lower()}",
        "capital": lambda s, o: f"{s} has capital {o}",
        "language": lambda s, o: f"The official language of {s} is {o}",
        "leaderTitle": lambda s, o: f"The leader title of {s} is {o}",
        "currency": lambda s, o: f"The currency of {s} is {o}",
        "ethnicGroup": lambda s, o: f"{s} is home to {o}",
        "origin": lambda s, o: f"{s} originates from {o}",
        "significantProject": lambda s, o: f"{s} is noted for {o}",
        "foundationPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} was founded in {o}",
        "demonym": lambda s, o: f"The people of {s} are called {o}",
        "numberOfRooms": lambda s, o: f"{s} contains {o} rooms",
        "keyPerson": lambda s, o: f"{s} lists {o} as a key person",
        "architecture": lambda s, o: f"{s} showcases {o.lower()}",
        "postalCode": lambda s, o: f"The postal code for {s} is {o}",
    }

    # Group phrases by subject to keep related facts together
    subject_phrases = {}
    for t in triples:
        pred = t.predicate
        obj = t.object
        phrase_func = templates.get(pred)
        if phrase_func:
            phrase = phrase_func(t.subject, obj)
        else:
            # Fallback to a generic rendering
            phrase = f"{t.subject} {pred.replace('_', ' ')} {obj}"
        subject_phrases.setdefault(t.subject, []).append(phrase)

    # Build sentences per subject then combine
    all_phrases = []
    for subj, phs in subject_phrases.items():
        if len(phs) == 1:
            all_phrases.append(phs[0])
        else:
            # join with commas and an 'and' before the last phrase
            combined = ", ".join(phs[:-1]) + ", and " + phs[-1]
            all_phrases.append(combined)

    sentence = "; ".join(all_phrases)
    if not sentence.endswith('.'):
        sentence += '.'
    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

Change 1: Replace:
  def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
      """
      Convert a list of triples into a single fluent sentence.
      Each triple is rendered as: "<subject> <predicate> <object>".
      Predicates are shown with spaces instead of underscores.
      Multiple triples are concatenated with commas and an 'and' before the last one.
      """
      if not triples:
          return ""
      # Build simple phrase for each triple
      phrases = [
          f"{t.subject} {t.predicate.replace('_', ' ')} {t.object}"
          for t in triples
      ]
      # Join phrases into a complex sentence
      if len(phrases) == 1:
          sentence = phrases[0]
      else:
          sentence = ", ".join(phrases[:-1]) + ", and " + phrases[-1]
      # Ensure sentence ends with a period
      if not sentence.endswith('.'):
          sentence += "."
      return sentence
